title: The Human Cell (Animal Cell)
description: An interactive tour of the microscopic building block of human life.

steps.speech: Welcome to the microscopic world of the human cell! This is an animal cell, the fundamental building block of your body. Let's explore its anatomy.
First, we have the cell membrane. It acts like a security gate, controlling what enters and leaves the cell to keep the internal environment stable.
At the center lies the nucleus. This is the command center of the cell, housing all of your DNA and genetic blueprints.
These orange, bean-shaped structures are the mitochondria. Often called the powerhouses of the cell, they generate the energy needed to power cellular functions.
Surrounding the nucleus is the endoplasmic reticulum, studded with tiny blue dots called ribosomes. Together, they act as a factory, synthesizing proteins.
Once proteins are made, they travel to the Golgi apparatus—the green stacked structure. It packages and ships proteins to their final destinations.
Finally, the red spheres are lysosomes. They contain enzymes that break down waste materials, acting as the cell's recycling center.
And there you have it! All of these organelles work together in perfect harmony to keep you alive.
